reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD as safe: it uses pinned checksums, standard extraction methods, and contains no dangerous operations during build/install. The skipped .deb file is a prebuilt binary which isn't auditable through static analysis.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped the binary .deb file. While binaries can't be fully audited, they should at least verify it's not an executable script masquerading as a binary.
No review of extracted files from the .deb (like the actual waveloggate binary) which execute at runtime
false_negatives: Neither model considered the risk of the prebuilt binary itself (e.g., potential malware in the unvetted binary from GitHub)
learnings: Improve binary handling: Detect if 'binary' files are actually scripts (using file command) before skipping
Prioritize reviewing extracted binaries/scripts that will be installed and executed
Flag prebuilt binaries as inherent risks even when packaging is safe
Add warning when packages fetch from GitHub releases (unvetted binaries)
